sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) man (perhaps so called from the idea of redness, compare דָּם [“The Arabs distinguish two races of men; one red, ruddy, which we call white, the other black.” Gesen. add. But both these races are sprung from Adam]). It has neither const, state, nor plural form; but it is very often used collect. to denote men, the human race, Gen 1:26, 27 6:1 Psa 68:19 76:11 Job 20:29 כָּל־אָדָם “all men,” Job 21:33. Sometimes it is put as a genitive after adjectives, as אֶבְיֹונֵי אָדָם “the needy of men,” i.e. needy men, Isa 29:19 comp. Hos 13:2 and with בְּ between, as בֹּגְדִים בְּאָדָם Pro 23:28.-Specially used-(a) for other men, the rest of mankind, as opposed to those in question; Jer 32:20, בְּיִשְׂרָאֵל וּבָאָדָם “in Israel and in other men,” Jud. 16:7 18:28 Psa 73:5 Isa 43:4.-(b) of common men, as opposed to those of better condition. So כְּאָדָם nach der (gemeinen) Menfchen Weife, Job 31:33 Hos 6:7 Psa 82:7. Opposed to אִישׁ viri (more noble), Isa 2:9 5:15 שָׂרִים Psa 82:7 comp. Isa 29:21, and in pl. בְּנֵי אִישׁ Psa 49:3 Pro 8:4.-(c) used of slaves, like נֶפֶשׁ Num 16:32.-(d) of soldiers, Kriegsmannfchaft, Isa 22:6 comp. אִישׁ No. 1, letter (h).

(2) a man, vir, i.q. אִישׁ Ecc 7:28, “a man (i.e. one emphatically, worthy of the name) I have found one of a thousand, but a woman in all their number I have not found.”

(3) any one, Lev 1:2 with a negative particle, no one, Job 32:21 comp. אִישׁ No. 4.

(4) [Adam], pr.n.-(a) of the first man made, Gen 2:7, seq. 3, 4. In these passages at least אָדָם assumes somewhat the nature of a proper name, as denoting the man as the only one of his kind; as הַבַּעַל Baal, lord; κατʼ ἐξ., הַשָּׂטָן Satan (Lehrg. p. 653, 654). Hence LXX. Ἀδάμ, Vulg. Adam.-(b) a town on the Jordan, Jos 3:16.

(5) בֶּן אָדָם with the art. “son of man;” used poet. for man, Num 23:19 Psa 8:5 80:18 Job 16:21 25:6 35:8 and very often in Ezekiel where the prophet is addressed by God, בֶּן אָדָם “son of man,” i.e. mortal, Eze 2:1, Eze 2:3 3:1 , Eze 3:3, Eze 3:4, 10 Eze 3:10 4:16 8:5 , Eze 8:6, Eze 8:8. More frequent also is the pl. בְּנֵי אָדָם men, Deu 32:8 Psa 11:4, etc.; with the art. בְּנֵי הָאָדָם 1Sa 26:19 1Ki 8:39 Psa 145:12 Ecc 1:13 comp. Syr. ܒܰܪܢܳܫܳܙ “son of men,” for man. [See בֵּן .]
